title: Design WebSocket subscription protocol for real-time push
markdown:
## Goal

Design and implement the server-side WebSocket subscription protocol. When a client subscribes to a URI prefix, the server pushes notifications for any matching writes. This replaces polling for real-time use cases.

## Background

Today, the WebSocket protocol (`WebSocketRequest`/`WebSocketResponse`) is strictly request/response. The server never initiates messages. For real-time apps (chat, live dashboards, collaborative editing), clients must poll, which is wasteful and adds latency.

The subscription protocol adds three new message types:
- `subscribe` -- client subscribes to a URI prefix
- `unsubscribe` -- client unsubscribes
- Server-initiated `notification` -- pushed when a matching write occurs

The server must track active subscriptions per connection and push notifications after successful `receive()` calls.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `WebSocketRequest.type` union extended with `"subscribe"` and `"unsubscribe"`
- [ ] New `WebSocketNotification` type: `{ type: "notification", uri: string, data: unknown, version?: number }`
- [ ] New `SubscriptionOptions` type: `{ prefix: string, id?: string }`
- [ ] Server-side `WebSocketHandler` (in `libs/b3nd-servers/`) tracks subscriptions per connection
- [ ] On `subscribe` request: registers prefix for that connection, returns success with subscription ID
- [ ] On `unsubscribe` request: removes subscription by ID
- [ ] After a successful `receive()` on the node, all connections with matching prefix subscriptions receive a notification
- [ ] Connection cleanup removes all subscriptions for that connection
- [ ] Subscription matching uses prefix comparison: subscription to `mutable://users` matches writes to `mutable://users/alice/profile`
- [ ] Maximum subscriptions per connection is configurable (default 100)
- [ ] Tests cover: subscribe, receive matching write, receive notification; unsubscribe stops notifications; connection cleanup

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `libs/b3nd-core/types.ts` to see existing `WebSocketRequest`, `WebSocketResponse` types
2. Add `"subscribe" | "unsubscribe"` to `WebSocketRequest.type`
3. Create `WebSocketNotification` type in `libs/b3nd-core/types.ts`
4. Read `libs/b3nd-servers/` to understand current WebSocket server setup (check if there is a ws.ts or if WS is handled in http.ts)
5. Create `libs/b3nd-servers/subscriptions.ts`:
   - `SubscriptionManager` class that tracks: `Map<WebSocket, Map<string, string>>` (connection -> subscriptionId -> prefix)
   - `subscribe(ws, prefix)` -> subscriptionId
   - `unsubscribe(ws, subscriptionId)` -> boolean
   - `notify(uri, data, version?)` -> iterates all connections, checks prefix match, sends notification
   - `removeConnection(ws)` -> cleanup
6. Integrate `SubscriptionManager` into the WebSocket server:
   - On `subscribe` message type: call `manager.subscribe()`
   - On `unsubscribe` message type: call `manager.unsubscribe()`
   - On `close` event: call `manager.removeConnection()`
   - After successful `receive()`: call `manager.notify(uri, data)`
7. Add tests for `SubscriptionManager` in `libs/b3nd-servers/subscriptions_test.ts`

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/b3nd-core/types.ts` -- extend WebSocketRequest type, add WebSocketNotification, SubscriptionOptions
- `libs/b3nd-servers/subscriptions.ts` -- new file: SubscriptionManager
- `libs/b3nd-servers/subscriptions_test.ts` -- new file: tests
- `libs/b3nd-servers/http.ts` or `libs/b3nd-servers/ws.ts` -- integrate SubscriptionManager

## Testing

```bash
deno test --allow-all libs/b3nd-servers/
```

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: Nothing (protocol design can proceed independently)
- **Blocks**: G1-A5-02 (WebSocketClient subscribe mode), G1-A5-03 (React hooks)
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Design WebSocket subscription protocol (server-push for URI prefix changes)
- [ ] Implement server-side: node tracks subscriptions, pushes matching messages
- [ ] Implement `WebSocketClient` subscribe mode (beyond current request/response)
- [ ] Add React hooks for subscriptions (`useSubscription(prefix)`)
